item.title: Off-field success overshadowed by Flying Fijians' on-field struggles
item.description: Off-field success overshadowed by Flying Fijians' on-field struggles

item.title: From military upbringing to rugby captain: Bitila Tawake will share her journey on fijivillage Her-Story on Wednesday at 7pm
item.description: From growing up in a strict military household to becoming captain of the Fijiana and the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women, Bitila Tawake's shares her story in this week's special episode of fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono , opening up about the experiences that shaped her into one of Fiji's leading female rugby players. Tawake reflects on balancing leadership with being a player, overcoming cr
item.content:encoded:
From growing up in a strict military household to becoming captain of the Fijiana and the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women, Bitila Tawake's shares her story in this week's special episode of fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono , opening up about the experiences that shaped her into one of Fiji's leading female rugby players.

Tawake reflects on balancing leadership with being a player, overcoming criticism in a male-dominated sport, missing family milestones in pursuit of her rugby dreams and the importance of inspiring the next generation of female athletes.

She also discusses the Drua Women's preparations for Saturday's Super Rugby Women semi-final against the Queensland Reds at Churchill Park in Lautoka, saying the team is focused on earning a place in the grand final.

Don't miss the interview with Tawake on fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono, at 7.30pm on Wednesday.

item.title: 25% TSLS allowance is only meant to assist students – Immanuel
item.description: The 25 percent allowance provided to students is intended as financial assistance and not to cover all of their expenses. This was highlighted by Minister for Finance Esrom Immanuel.
item.content:encoded:
The 25 percent allowance provided to students is intended as financial assistance and not to cover all of their expenses.

This was highlighted by Minister for Finance Esrom Immanuel.

While responding to questions from fijivillage News on concerns that the allowance may not be enough to cover upfront costs such as rent, accommodation and textbooks, Immanuel says TSLS normally consults with tertiary institutions and universities before such decisions are made.

He says the main role of the Tertiary Scholarships and Loans Service is to ensure students' programmes are funded, while the allowance is provided as additional assistance.

Immanuel also reveals that more students than expected accessed TSLS funding in the first semester, resulting in some of the funding allocated for the second semester already being utilised.

He says the number of students who enrolled in the first semester doubled compared to what was expected.

Immanuel says student numbers are usually expected to be spread across the first, second and third semesters, but the higher intake in the first semester meant some of the funding for the second semester had already been used.

When asked whether students who have not returned to continue their studies had contributed to financial pressure on TSLS, Immanuel says students are not currently required to repay their loans and the issue is related to the allocation of funding.

On the reduction in government funding for Pacific Polytech, Immanuel says allocations are determined independently by the Higher Education Commission using its own methodology.

He says government grants are only one source of funding for institutions, as they also receive scholarship payments from TSLS for enrolled students.

Immanuel adds the increase in the number of students pursuing higher education is important as Fiji continues to face demand for skilled workers across a number of industries.

He says this is evident from the need to bring workers from overseas to fill vacancies.

item.title: 3-year-old child dies in alleged drowning as search continues for other two victims
item.description: A 3-year-old child is among three alleged drowning cases currently under investigation, while the search continues for a missing 20-year-old man in Sigatoka. Police say the child was found floating in a river near Lawaki Village in Tailevu on Friday afternoon after the incident was reported at the Korovou Police Station.
item.content:encoded:
A 3-year-old child is among three alleged drowning cases currently under investigation, while the search continues for a missing 20-year-old man in Sigatoka.

Police say the child was found floating in a river near Lawaki Village in Tailevu on Friday afternoon after the incident was reported at the Korovou Police Station.

The second case involves a 20-year-old man who went missing after he was allegedly swept away by strong currents while diving off Naevuevu Village in Sigatoka on Friday.

The incident was reported by a 15-year-old who was with him, and search efforts have so far been unsuccessful .

In the third case, the body of a 68-year-old farmer was recovered from waters off Yadua in Sigatoka on Saturday afternoon by a joint search team who is believed to have drowned after his boat capsized.

Investigations into all three cases are continuing.

item.title: Woman charged with trafficking after allegedly sending woman to Egypt
item.description: A 42-year-old woman has been charged with one count of trafficking in persons for allegedly facilitating a 51-year-old woman's tra

item.title: More former Vatukoula mine workers receive compensation
New 9.1km access road to improve lives in Ba interior
item.description: A new farm access road linking communities in the interior of Ba is expected to improve the lives of residents by cutting travel times, reducing transport costs and providing easier access to markets and essential services. Work is continuing on the 9.1 kilometre Navilawa–Yaloku Access Road, which is being funded by the Coalition Government through the Ministry of Rural and Maritime Development an
item.content:encoded:
A new farm access road linking communities in the interior of Ba is expected to improve the lives of residents by cutting travel times, reducing transport costs and providing easier access to markets and essential services.

Work is continuing on the 9.1 kilometre Navilawa–Yaloku Access Road, which is being funded by the Coalition Government through the Ministry of Rural and Maritime Development and Disaster Management's Rural Outer Island (ROI) Development Programme.

The $200,000 project will connect communities between Nadi and Ba, providing farmers with a more efficient route to transport their produce to markets.

Once completed, the road is expected to reduce travel time between Sabeto, Nadi and Ba from about two hours to just 40 minutes.

The improved road network is also expected to lower transportation costs, enhance road safety and create new economic opportunities for rural communities while improving access to essential services.

The Ministry says work on the project is progressing as part of the Government's ongoing investment in rural infrastructure aimed at strengthening connectivity and supporting economic development in remote communities.

item.title: Issuing of search warrants should be with the courts, not JPs - Rakiraki villager

item.title: Two dead, including a 10-year-old child, in tragic Laucala Beach blaze
item.description: A devastating fire at Laucala Beach this morning has claimed the lives of two people, including a 10-year-old child.The National F
